After cleansing myself of 50k and two tent sleeps of sweat and stank, i reached for a comfy t-shirt. I pulled out my Ragnar Cape Cod tee, received from a fantastic group trip I did with some good friends in 2018. The shirt signifies that we accomplished something rad, and that layer of accomplishment is soothing today.

Today was the start of Bad Beaver Ultra Day 2, and I did not start it. The recent knee injury that started about 3 weeks ago did reappear about 2/3 of the way into Day 1, and I had to hobble to the finish line.

It's really, REALLY hard calling it quits. Everyone at the race is so supportive and full of an endless supply of strength and encouragement. But after feeling that growing, acute pain for over 3 hours yesterday, and knowing from experience it will take many days to be able to put a reasonably pain-free 5k behind me, attempting tackle the 70k today would have led to a lot of damage.

As I mentally transitioned from runner to spectator, it was painful watching the runners go this morning. But the transition is now complete, and I plan on being super stoked cheering them all in as they cross the finish line Saturday morning in Chelsea. Some super strong and great folks they all are, was I'm grateful to spend the time I did with them.

Learned so much! Always learning! I had everything dialed in, and many things I thought would be an issue, were not. GI was amazing, calories and electrolytes in full swing and plentiful. Solid food was good too. My pack was great, little sway. Some chafing early in the run solved quickly with splax. Ankles and achilles felt strong. Zero cramping, muscles felt (and still feel) good. Zero blisters, one big bruise on my left foot likely due to laces being too tight as my feet swelled. Back is not bad considering the 7kg i started with on it. All was good... but my left knee. Time to get to work on THAT.

Huge ups to the @3beaversracing and @rayzahab for putting on a REALLY special event, and being so supportive and wise. And to all my cheerers for giving me the strength to get as far as I did. So happy i was a part of this event, and i hope to be back when the event is back in TWO years âð¼

2018 was a great year for me. A year of experience, love, and manifestation.

For 2019, I'm hoping forÂ a year of realization. The first rather obvious realization, is manifestation isn't just idling away and thinking really damn hard - thinking hard is of course a big part of it, but it's also actually doing shit. Usually small bits of shit you donât even know are related to what your thinking of, and sometimes hard and big pieces of shit. A bit of bad shit, some great shit, and definitely some uncomfortable shit (spoiler - thatâs the best shit). And i've done some shit, for some time, and now is the time to realize some of the results of that shit. Business bob is gonna chill in 2019. Today Iâm welcoming in his replacement, sabbatical bob.

I've worked very hard at my job and at my career for the past 17.5 years, and am now in the ridiculously fortunate position of taking 1 year "on" to work on myself. Whatever the pump that means. Initial things involve trail running, skateboarding, and reading books. Things i've wanted to do so much more of, i'm going to have time for. Iâm so stoked that Ania and I are going to start the year by exploring the southern states for a month in a van, and we'll let our whimsy and dreams guide us after that. A bit nervous to see what fills in all the business gaps when the work dreams stop (they certainly haven't yet!), but also beaming with excitement and anticipation at the same time.

Action on McLeod Street!! As I was finishing my run I could hear sirens start to overtake the sound of my tunes. A black sedan comes flying up Percy THE WRONG WAY with a cop a few hundred feet back. The sedan dodges a few cars and makes a sharp left onto McLeod, and the cop makes its way behind 10 seconds later. I rush up to McLeod to see the black sedan parked about halfway up, and the man running down the street with the cop flying out of his car hot in pursuit! Eventually I could see him tackling the dude and thrashing around. He eventually settled him as another cop blocks the other side from Bronson and emerges to help his colleague. May not look like much but my favorite meal of the trip. We stopped at a small hotel outside in a town near the castle for lunch. This Wiener schnitzel started with a salad of great pickled carrots, radish, cabbage, as well as fresh lettuce and light dressing. The schnitzel was perfect - but what really set it apart was the accompaniments. On the side was perfect fried potatoes, red onions, and bacon. Featured here in the top is lemon, capers, and a single anchovy. At the bottom is delicious cranberry sauce atop a slice of orange. I cleaned everything, and the combos of all parts played SO well together. The traditional German food I had before this meal was very very simple. But this is my favorite - it's simple, but detailed. And most delicious!!
